visual_checks: Look for help mechanisms on the page — is there a 'Contact Us' link, a live chat widget, a phone number, a help/FAQ link, or a self-help option such as a chatbot?
If multiple pages are available, check whether the help mechanism appears in the same relative location on each page (e.g., always in the footer, always in the top-right header area)
Check whether the order of multiple help options is consistent across pages — if one page shows 'Chat | Email | Phone', do other pages maintain that same order?
Look for help mechanisms that appear inside forms or transactional flows — are they in the same relative position as help mechanisms on other pages?
Identify if the help mechanism is behind a consistent icon or label (e.g., a question-mark icon, a 'Help' button) across all pages
pass_conditions: Help mechanisms (contact info, chat widget, FAQ link, phone number) appear in the same relative order within each page where they are present
If a help feature like live chat appears on multiple pages, it is positioned in the same relative location on each page
Multiple help contact methods (phone, email, chat) maintain the same relative order across pages
A self-help mechanism (chatbot, FAQ search) appears in a consistent position and is invoked the same way on each page
The help mechanism may differ in visual appearance across pages (e.g., expanded vs. collapsed) but its relative position in the page structure remains consistent
fail_conditions: A 'Contact Support' link appears in the header on some pages but only in the footer on other pages, with no consistent relative position
Multiple help options (Chat, Phone, Email) appear in different orderings on different pages (e.g., 'Chat | Phone | Email' on one page, 'Email | Chat | Phone' on another)
A help chat widget is present on the homepage and product pages but entirely absent from the checkout flow where users are most likely to need it
The help mechanism changes location based on page template — in the right sidebar on content pages but at the bottom of forms on transaction pages
na_conditions: The page contains no help mechanisms (no contact information, no chat widget, no FAQ link, no support phone number, no self-help tool)
The page is a single standalone page, and help mechanism consistency across pages cannot be assessed
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Only one page was captured, making it impossible to assess help mechanism consistency across pages
The presence or absence of help mechanisms could not be determined from the available screenshots and DOM data
common_mistakes: Placing the live chat widget in the bottom-right corner on most pages but moving it to the bottom-left on certain pages
Showing help contact options (phone, email, chat) in a different order on the Contact page compared to the footer on other pages
Removing the help mechanism entirely from checkout or login pages where users may need it most
Using a 'Help' label in the navigation on some pages but a question-mark icon with no label on other pages, positioned in a different location
A FAQ link that appears in the main navigation on the homepage but is moved to a sidebar on interior pages

examples.pass: Every page on the site has a 'Help' button in the top-right of the header that opens a support panel with 'Live Chat | Email Support | FAQ' in that order — consistent position and consistent ordering on every page
examples.fail: The homepage has 'Live Chat | Phone | Email' help options in the footer, but the product pages show 'Email | Phone' in a sidebar with no chat option, and the checkout page has 'Phone | Live Chat' in a different order in the header
examples.partial: The help chat widget appears consistently in the bottom-right corner on most pages, but on two pages generated by an older template, the chat widget is absent and the only help option is a 'Contact Us' footer link in a different position

plain_meaning: If a set of pages offers help mechanisms (such as human contact details, a contact form, a self-help option, or an automated chatbot), and those mechanisms are repeated across multiple pages, they must appear in the same order relative to other page content on each page. The user can initiate a change and that is fine.
scope_applies_to: Contact phone numbers or email repeated across pages
Contact forms or 'Contact us' links repeated across pages
FAQ or self-help links repeated across pages
Chatbot widgets or automated help mechanisms repeated across pages
Help-center entry points repeated across pages
scope_does_not_apply_to: Pages that do not contain any help mechanism at all
Pages where help appears on only one page of the set
Visual styling differences when order is preserved
Content areas that are not help-oriented
borderline_calibration: Only the RELATIVE order to other page content matters.
A floating chat widget that consistently appears in a screen corner on every page satisfies the rule because its position relative to content is consistent.
Do not flag when no help mechanism is present at all (Not Applicable).
Do not flag when help appears on only a single page (no repetition).
